```{r}
library(MASS)

n_draws <- 1000
draws <- mvrnorm(n_draws, mu = c(0, 0), Sigma = matrix(c(1, 0, 0, 1), nrow = 2))
head(draws)
```

```{r}
# install.packages(c("MASS", "ggplot2"))
library(MASS)
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(123)

# Mean vector
mu <- c(0, 0)

# Covariance matrix
Sigma <- matrix(c(
    1, 2,
    2, 5
), nrow = 2)

# Generate samples
x <- MASS::mvrnorm(n = 2000, mu = mu, Sigma = Sigma)
df <- data.frame(x1 = x[, 1], x2 = x[, 2])

# Scatter plot
ggplot(df, aes(x = x1, y = x2)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.25) +
    coord_equal() +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
        title = "2D Multivariate Normal Sample",
        x = "X1",
        y = "X2"
    )

# install.packages("mvtnorm")
library(mvtnorm)

# Grid of values
x_seq <- seq(-4, 4, length.out = 100)
y_seq <- seq(-4, 4, length.out = 100)
grid <- expand.grid(x1 = x_seq, x2 = y_seq)

# Density at each grid point
grid$z <- mvtnorm::dmvnorm(grid, mean = mu, sigma = Sigma)

# Base R contour plot
z_matrix <- matrix(grid$z, nrow = length(x_seq), byrow = FALSE)

contour(x_seq, y_seq, z_matrix,
    xlab = "X1", ylab = "X2",
    main = "Contour of 2D Multivariate Normal"
)

persp(x_seq, y_seq, z_matrix,
    theta = 30, phi = 25, expand = 0.6,
    col = "lightblue",
    xlab = "X1", ylab = "X2", zlab = "Density",
    main = "3D Surface of Multivariate Normal Density"
)

library(ggplot2)
library(mvtnorm)

x_seq <- seq(-4, 4, length.out = 150)
y_seq <- seq(-4, 4, length.out = 150)
grid <- expand.grid(x1 = x_seq, x2 = y_seq)
grid$z <- mvtnorm::dmvnorm(grid, mean = mu, sigma = Sigma)

ggplot(grid, aes(x = x1, y = x2, z = z)) +
  geom_contour() +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Multivariate Normal Contours")
```
